mappings.rationale: Examines African American religious movements including the Nation of Islam and Black churches, directly relevant to 829.B on Negro cults and 829.E.3.
Rastafari exemplifies a new religious movement shaped by the impact of dominant cultures on African diaspora peoples, relevant to 829.A and 829.B.
Mormonism stands as a major new religious movement reflecting the impact of Christianity on American religious life, relevant to 829.A.
Explores the foundational scripture of the Latter-day Saint movement, a key text for understanding Mormonism as a new religious development relevant to 829.A.
Surveys diverse new religious movements in America including the Nation of Islam, Jainism, and emergent spiritual communities, relevant to 829.A-B.
Traces modern Islamic movements and their global spread, relevant to 829.E on religions and movements of Islamic origin.
Islamic sects and offshoots provide essential context for understanding the Baha'i faith and Druze religion discussed in 829.E.1-2.
Explores modern neo-pagan revivals including Blavatsky's theosophy, the Golden Dawn, and shamanism, relevant to 829.C on theosophical groups and 829.F on revivals.
Modern witchcraft movements, especially Wicca, are directly relevant to 829.F.1 on witchcraft and black magic in modern civilizations.
Explores modern magical practices and occult beliefs, relevant to 829.F.1 on black magic and Satanism in modern civilizations.
Examines superstitious beliefs and divination practices that persist in modern life, relevant to 829.F.2 on prophecy, divination, and astrology.
Fundamentalist movements across religious traditions represent a powerful response to modernity, relevant to 829.A on movements reflecting the impact of dominant cultures.
Pentecostalism's charismatic worship and healing practices make it a significant newer movement, relevant to 829.A and 829.F.3 on healing cults.
Newer evangelical movements and megachurches represent important new religious developments within Christianity, relevant to 829.A.
Explores modern spiritual movements outside traditional religion including transpersonal psychology, relevant to 829.D on spiritualist groups and 829.F.3.
Modern druidism represents a revival of ancient religious practices, relevant to 829.F on residues or revivals of ancient beliefs.
Freemasonry is a fraternal movement with quasi-religious initiation rites and theosophical connections, relevant to 829.C.
